title: Recognising AML with KMT2A rearrangement from integrated laboratory findings
learning_objectives: Recognise morphology and immunophenotype of KMT2A-rearranged AML
Understand the role of KMT2A rearrangements in AML classification
Integrate flow cytometry, cytogenetics and molecular testing
clinical_vignette: A 24-year-old woman presents with fever, gingival hypertrophy and leukocytosis. Bone marrow examination demonstrates acute leukaemia with prominent monocytic differentiation. Rapid molecular cytogenetic testing is requested.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Numerous monoblasts and promonocytes with folded nuclei and abundant basophilic cytoplasm.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow extensively infiltrated by monoblasts with monocytic maturation.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: AML with monocytic differentiation; NG2 expression raises suspicion for KMT2A rearrangement.

cytogenetics: FISH demonstrates a KMT2A rearrangement. Conventional karyotype identifies t(9;11)(p21.3;q23.3).
molecular_genetics: KMT2A::MLLT3 fusion confirmed. NPM1 and FLT3-ITD negative.
question: Which flow cytometric finding most strongly supports suspicion of a KMT2A-rearranged AML before cytogenetic confirmation?
correct_answer: B. NG2 (CSPG4) expression with monocytic immunophenotype
explanation: KMT2A-rearranged AML commonly shows monocytic differentiation with CD64/CD11c and may express NG2 (CSPG4). Definitive diagnosis requires demonstration of the rearrangement.

why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Suggests APL rather than KMT2A AML.

frcpath_pearl: Marked monocytic differentiation with gingival hypertrophy should prompt testing for KMT2A rearrangements.
take_home_message: Integrate morphology, immunophenotype and genetics when diagnosing KMT2A-rearranged AML.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
